key_insight: TO-Master uses an LLM agent to turn topology optimization into a conversational, tool-driven workflow that automates model setup, solver orchestration, and postprocessing.
engineering_relevance: It lowers the expert barrier for finite-element-based topology optimization, making compliance, thermal, and stress-constrained design studies more accessible for engineering workflows.
method: Natural-language prompts plus optional mesh/geometry/image inputs are parsed by an LLM agent.
The agent selects tools to build FEM TO models, verify meshes and boundary conditions, and launch sensitivity-based optimization.
Supports 2D/3D compliance minimization, thermal conduction, multiple load cases, stress constraints, and engineering geometries.
evidence_signal: The paper reports benchmark reproduction and more complex engineering case studies, along with optimized fields, convergence histories, and interactive artifacts.
watch_item: Robust performance depends on good instruction design and tool-usage guidance, so ambiguous prompts may still require careful oversight.
paper_title: TO-Master: an LLM-agent framework for automated topology optimization
